titel: A requirement for pre-application notification for parental leave benefits has been abolished.
html:
The proposal suggests a change in the Social Security Code of Laws, which would eliminate the requirement to notify the authorities before applying for parental leave. Removing this notification requirement would make it easier and simpler for parents to plan their parental leave and apply for parental benefits. This change could also contribute to more efficient administration.

The proposal also includes a transitional provision related to a change in the Social Security Code of Laws that was made in conjunction with the repeal of the Act (1994:451) on intensive monitoring with electronic control, effective January 1, 2026. This provision concerns who is covered by state personal injury insurance.

The proposal also includes a correction in Chapter 13, Section 25 of the Social Security Code of Laws regarding the benefit period for temporary parental leave in certain situations.

The legislative change regarding the elimination of the notification requirement is proposed to take effect on July 1, 2026. The other legislative changes are proposed to take effect on May 31, 2026, but will apply retroactively from January 1, 2026.

Proposed Parliamentary Decision

Prop. 2025/26:117

Government's proposal:

  1. The Parliament adopts the government's proposal for an amendment to the Social Security Code of Laws.
  2. The Parliament adopts the government's proposal for an amendment to the Social Security Code of Laws.

Prop. 2025/26:117 2
tldr: It will become easier for parents to receive parental leave benefits, as the requirement to first register their leave will be removed. The proposed change is scheduled to take effect from July 2026.
lattlast:
The government wants to change the rules in the Social Security Code, which is the law that governs our social security programs. The new proposal means that parents will no longer need to make a notification before applying for parental leave benefits. This makes it easier to plan one's leave and saves time for the authorities.

There are also other, smaller changes in the proposal. Some of the changes concern the protection of individuals in cases of injury and how long compensation can be received for temporary parental leave in certain cases. These changes are intended to correct inaccuracies in the law.

The new rules regarding the elimination of the notification requirement for parental leave are proposed to come into effect on July 1, 2026. The other changes in the law are proposed to come into effect on May 31, 2026, but they should begin to be applied from January 1, 2026.
